name: East Riverside Park
description: Come play pickleball at East Riverside Park in Greer, SC! There are 18 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Players have access to lights and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Fluor Pickleball Courts
description: Employees must sign activity waiver before using the courts (one-time). Employees must pick up the court key to unlock the gate to play.

name: Fourth Presbyterian Church
description: Come play pickleball at Fourth Presbyterian Church in Greenville, SC! There are 3 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are private.

name: Gower Estates Park
description: Gower Estates Park is a place to play pickleball in Greenville, SC. There are 10 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights.

name: Greenville Country Club
description: Greenville Country Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Greenville, SC. There are 4 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include restrooms and lights. A membership is required to play.

name: Greenville Pickleball Academy
description: Greenville Pickleball Academy is a place to play pickleball in Greenville, SC. There are 9 indoor concrete and acrylic courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. You can reserve the courts. Players have access to restrooms, water, youth programming, lights, trainers or lessons, food, and a pro shop or equipment store. A one-time fee is required to play.
fees: Fee required — Pay to play, memberships available but not required

name: Greenville Tennis Club
description: Greenville Tennis Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Simpsonville, SC. The court surface is hard. The court lines are permanent. Amenities include lights and restrooms. A membership is required to play.

name: Mcpherson Park
description: There are 2 tennis courts but only one is marked for pickleball

name: Northside Park
description: Northside Park is a place to play pickleball in Greenville, SC. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights. You can’t reserve the courts.

name: Pavilion Recreation Complex
description: Only courts are indoors, outdoor tennis courts are NOT marked for pickleball.

name: Phillis Wheatley Community Center
description: Come play pickleball at Phillis Wheatley Community Center in Greenville, SC! There are 3 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free.

name: Pickle Yard
description: Pickle Yard is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Mauldin, SC. There are 6 indoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include food, restrooms, and trainers or lessons. A one-time fee is required to play.
hours: Sun–Thurs • 6am – 9pm Fri & Sat • 6am – 10pm     © 2026 Pickle Yard Mauldin | All Rights Reserved

name: PKL Park
description: PKL Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Greenville, SC. There are 8 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A one-time fee is required to play. Amenities include food, restrooms, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, and trainers or lessons. Reservations are available.
open_play_schedule:
Free Open play 11:00 AM-2:00 PM 
Reserve a court 8:00 AM-10:00 PM
fees:
Fee required — $15 per court per hour M-F 8:00AM-4:00PM
$30 per court per hour After 4:00PM and all day Saturday-Sunday

name: Ray Hopkins Senior Center
description: Ray Hopkins Senior Center is a place to play pickleball in Greenville, SC. There are 3 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water. The courts are free.

name: Simpsonville Activity & Senior Center
description: Ages 50 and up only. One court is designated for beginners. Walk-ins welcome.

name: Southside Park
description: Southside Park is a place to play pickleball in Greenville, SC. There are 12 outdoor hard and carpet courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, and lights. Reservations are not available.

name: SportsClub Greenville
description: Come play pickleball at SportsClub Greenville in Greenville, SC! There are 14 outdoor clay and hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. A membership is required to play. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, food, a pro shop or equipment store, water, and lights.

name: Sterling Community Center
description: Come play pickleball at Sterling Community Center in Greenville, SC! There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are private. Amenities include restrooms and water.

name: The Picklr Greenville SC
description: Soon to open!

name: Thornblade Country Club
description: Come play pickleball at Thornblade Country Club in Greer, SC! There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to lights, restrooms, and a pro shop or equipment store. A membership is required to play. Reservations are not available.

name: Timmons Park
description: Timmons Park is a place to play pickleball in Greenville, SC. There are 8 outdoor hard and asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, and lights. The courts cannot be reserved.
open_play_schedule: Every Tuesday, starting on March 14-May 2, all the courts will be closed from 5pm-9pm for league play.

name: Trinity Bible Church
description: Trinity Bible Church is a place to play pickleball in Greer, SC. There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free.

name: Tryon Recreation Center
description: Members have the privilege of reserving court time based on the availability of lessons, leagues, tournaments, etc.
open_play_schedule: Courts 1-2 always open play/walk up. M-F Courts 1-6 are scheduled open play. No memberships required to play here.
